title: succisa stellata
intro: Succisa stellata, also known as wood betony or sticky mouse-ear, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small blue-purple flowers and hairy leaves. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include acting as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. The health benefits of Succisa stellata include reducing fever and inflammation, treating wounds and skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Succisa stellata can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to promote relaxation and relieve stress.
